text: Existentialism (19th-20th centuries) asserts that 'existence precedes essence' — humans are born without predetermined purpose and must create their own meaning through choices and actions. Key thinkers: Kierkegaard, Nietzsche, Heidegger, Sartre, Camus. Emphasizes individual freedom, responsibility, and the anxiety of authentic existence.
Camus: The Myth of Sisyphus — life is absurd (meaningless), but we must imagine Sisyphus happy.
